stories.body: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it struck 85 American military installations in Bahrain and Kuwait in retaliation for wide-ranging US raids on Iranian territory, accusing Washington of violating last month’s memorandum of understanding by reimposing oil sanctions that had temporarily allowed crude, petrochemical and petroleum sales until 21 August; Tehran’s negotiators now argue the accord is void, while former US President Donald Trump berated NATO allies for insufficient support as tensions around the Strait of Hormuz intensified.
stories.headline: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard strikes US-linked sites in Bahrain and Kuwait after sanctions snapback

stories.body: US Central Command said American forces destroyed Iranian surveillance and radar sites in Goruk and on Qeshm Island after shooting down four drones aimed at maritime traffic near the Strait of Hormuz, with Washington calling Tehran’s attacks on three commercial vessels an egregious ceasefire breach; Iran, accused of blocking the vital waterway and demanding fees for ship passage, insisted the memorandum governing the strait leaves it in charge with Oman and rejected proposals for a new joint authority even as Qatar warned Tehran it would bear full legal responsibility for the tanker hits.
stories.headline: US strikes Iranian coastal radar sites after drones threaten Hormuz shipping

stories.body: Russia’s defense ministry told President Vladimir Putin that its forces have seized the strategic city of Kostiantynivka in eastern Ukraine and are pushing toward Lyman while expanding security zones to blunt Ukrainian long-range strikes, with top commanders showcasing footage of troops raising flags over shattered infrastructure even as Kyiv declined to confirm the loss and Western mediation remains stalled amid the broader Iran conflict.
stories.headline: Russia claims capture of Kostiantynivka and eyes further gains in Donetsk

stories.body: Iranian officials reaffirmed support for Hezbollah and said any broader ceasefire with the United States must include an Israeli pullout from southern Lebanon, arguing the regional war that erupted after February’s US-Israeli strikes on Iran cannot end while Israel continues cross-border operations; Hezbollah has stepped up attacks near Beaufort Castle, Lebanese authorities report Israeli airstrikes across the south, and Tehran warns Israel against threatening Beirut while insisting that control over Hormuz shipping lanes and sanctions relief remain core demands in ongoing negotiations.
stories.headline: Tehran links wider US ceasefire deal to Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon

stories.body: Five astronauts briefly sheltered in their Crew Dragon spacecraft after an air leak on Russia’s Zvezda module doubled from one to two pounds of atmosphere per day, but NASA rescinded evacuation orders once Roscosmos sealed one crack, worked on a second and paused a disputed repair plan that had prompted the safe-haven command; the partners continue to assess the risk on the 27-year-old station, which has never been evacuated, while US lawmakers debate extending its life to 2032 amid broader competition with China in orbit.
stories.headline: NASA calls off ISS evacuation after Russia plugs air leak

stories.body: The Senate voted 52-47 along party lines to send a $70 billion immigration enforcement package to the House without stripping former President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, a pot critics label a slush fund for allies; Republican leaders insisted the Justice Department has paused the program even as moderates sought binding limits, while Democrats decried new deportation cash just as campaigns heat up for November’s midterms.
stories.headline: Senate passes $70 billion DHS enforcement boost while Trump ‘anti-weaponization’ fund survives

stories.body: The Department of Homeland Security said Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican national targeted for living in the US without legal status, died at a hospital after an ICE officer fired when Salgado allegedly ignored commands, tried to flee in a vehicle and rammed an agent during a Tuesday operation in Houston; the shooting, now under FBI review, triggered calls from Democratic officials and immigrant advocates for an independent investigation as the Trump administration accelerates mass deportation sweeps.
stories.headline: ICE officer fatally shoots Mexican man during Houston arrest operation

stories.body: National and Maine Democratic leaders, major fundraising committees and progressive groups cut ties with Senate candidate Graham Platner after a woman alleged he forced her to have sex in 2021, prompting Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, DSCC chair Kirsten Gillibrand and others to demand he withdraw so the party can name a replacement before state deadlines, while Platner denied the accusation but paused events and said he is weighing his next steps as supporters search for alternative nominees.
stories.headline: Democrats abandon Maine Senate nominee Graham Platner after assault allegation

stories.body: The Iranian American Legal Defense Fund and Public Citizen sued in federal court alleging that since March 2025 the Trump administration’s immigration agencies have relayed sensitive asylum application information on detained Iranians to Tehran via meetings arranged through Pakistan’s embassy, facilitating pressure for deportations despite regulations forbidding disclosure of claims by converts, LGBTQ people or protesters; DHS denies sharing records even as plaintiffs cite reports of detainees forced into interviews with Iranian officials.
stories.headline: Lawsuit accuses US of sharing Iranian asylum seekers’ confidential data with Tehran

stories.body: Minister Félix Bolaños defended pending legislation to bar political parties, unions and their affiliates from acting as popular prosecutors, arguing that far-right groups such as Manos Limpias and Hazte Oír have abused the mechanism in cases targeting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife and brother, eroding trust in the judiciary at a time when a 40dB poll for El País and Cadena SER found 65% of Spaniards believe lawfare exists, with even higher concern among progressive voters.
stories.headline: Spanish government pushes to curb politicized popular prosecutions

stories.body: Podemos leader Ione Belarra warned the party will oppose any housing decree that trades the planned extension of rent caps for fiscal incentives to landlords, a concession the PSOE is considering to secure Junts’ votes, underscoring the governing coalition’s struggle to balance right-leaning and left-leaning demands as it assembles a broader legislative agenda.
stories.headline: Podemos threatens to block rent control extension if landlords get tax breaks

stories.body: The European Parliament cleared €120.55 million from the EU Solidarity Fund to help Spain recover from 2025 wildfires that devastated Castilla y León, Galicia, Extremadura and Asturias, causing eight deaths and an estimated €4.3 billion in losses, with lawmakers citing severe environmental, social and economic harm and noting that evacuated rural communities will receive part of the 12% share Spain now takes from the annual recovery budget.
stories.headline: EU approves €120 million solidarity aid for Spain’s 2025 wildfire damage

stories.body: A sinkhole about eight meters wide and four meters deep opened near the Putxet section of Barcelona’s L9 metro works, collapsing part of a restaurant’s patio and lavatory and prompting authorities to evacuate residents from eight buildings—93 homes—on Rubinstein and Teodora Lamadrid streets; Mayor Jaume Collboni said evacuees must spend at least one night elsewhere while emergency crews inject concrete, let escorted visits retrieve essentials and monitor stability as the tunneling machine sits roughly a dozen meters from the void.
stories.headline: Sinkhole linked to L9 works forces evacuation of 93 homes in Sant Gervasi

stories.body: Catalan rural agents raised Collserola Natural Park to level 3 of the Alfa wildfire danger plan, triggering Barcelona’s municipal wildfire alert as weeks of heat, a rain-fueled undergrowth surge and restricted forest management due to African swine fever leave 8,000 hectares highly combustible; authorities have banned spark-generating work within 500 meters of forest, reinforced surveillance towers and patrols, and urged residents to respect prohibitions on burning waste or using barbecues.
stories.headline: Collserola Park placed on top wildfire alert amid heatwave and fuel buildup

stories.body: Authorities said the Sentmenat wildfire that began when an excavator ignited on a rural property has already burned nearly 160 hectares, blackening the point-zero area and destroying equipment while Bombers and volunteer rural defense groups focus on the right flank to keep flames from moving into Vallès Oriental and the Sant Llorenç del Munt i l’Obac park during a critical afternoon window of heat and sea breezes; Interior Minister Núria Parlon warned of tough hours ahead as roads remain closed and residents await permission to return.
stories.headline: Sentmenat wildfire ravages ignition zone as crews fight spread toward Vallès

stories.body: Barcelona will begin work in early 2027 on a €36.6 million overhaul of the derelict Llac building at the Tres Xemeneies complex to create a 24/7 Centro de Coordinación Operativa y de Emergencias that doubles current capacity for Guardia Urbana, Bombers, Mossos and medical command posts, with an 18-month construction timeline followed by specialized fit-out so the facility can open in 2028 as part of a broader redevelopment that also unlocks housing and public amenities around the current Lleida Street site.
stories.headline: Barcelona approves €36.6 million emergency coordination hub at Tres Xemeneies
